name: Toasted Coconut Protein Cheesecake
description: Toasted-coconut variant of the vanilla base. Coconut runs throughout the filling (45 g toasted, fine-ground, beaten in) plus a 20 g toasted-coconut topping; crustless to stay lean-ish. Coconut is the only flavor source (the oil is neutral) so it is toasted hard for aroma. Macro note: coconut is ~64% fat, so this runs richer than the base - about 183 cal/slice, ratio ~12.3 vs the base 130/9.1. To-make; a tsp of coconut extract (0 cal) is the planned next lever to deepen flavor and drop the shredded amount.

payload.instructionText: Toast 65 g shredded coconut in a dry skillet over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until golden and fragrant (it colors fast then burns - watch it). Cool. Grind 45 g to a fine meal (spice grinder or food processor) and beat it into the filling with the wet ingredients in step 3 so the coconut disperses throughout; reserve the other 20 g for the topping.
Once the cheesecake is fully chilled, scatter the reserved 20 g toasted coconut evenly over the top just before serving (keeps it crisp).
Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.

payload.text: Shredded coconut goes from golden to burnt in under a minute — use medium heat and stir constantly; pull the skillet off the heat the moment it smells fragrant, as residual pan heat will darken it another shade.
Once the 2 eggs and egg white go in, mix on low just until smooth — overbeating whips in air that makes the cheesecake puff, crack, and sink, and with a lean cottage-cheese base it also turns grainy.

Grind the 45 g filling coconut to a fine meal (spice grinder, 15–20 sec) before beating it in — coarse shreds leave sandy pockets; a fine meal disperses evenly and gives a smooth coconut flavor throughout every bite.

Pull at 155–160F center or when there's only a slight wobble in the middle — chasing a fully set center with 2 scoops of protein powder and cottage cheese will result in a dry, rubbery slice. It firms dramatically overnight in the fridge.
Add the 20 g toasted topping right before serving, not before refrigerating — coconut absorbs moisture in the fridge and loses its crunch within a few hours of contact with the cheesecake surface.
